regime_fall.current_status: The US-Iran ceasefire formally expired on April 19, 2026, with both sides conducting military escalations while diplomatic talks reconvene in Pakistan on April 21; a US naval blockade enforces economic strangulation as Iran's Interim Leadership Council struggles to navigate succession amid hardline pressure and severe internal instability.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Trump stated the ceasefire was extended but deemed further extension 'highly unlikely,' while JD Vance and US officials prepare for negotiations in Pakistan, even as Iran maintains a 'shoot-on-sight directive' against US personnel and the military conducts combat readiness exercises.
